timelines.notes: Fetched 2026-06-20. Aggregated across Al Jazeera's Bangladesh coverage (per user instruction; no single AJ article carries 15+ dated entries on the minority-attacks subtopic). Contributing articles: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/6/attacks-on-hindus-in-bangladesh-spark-fears-after-hasina-ouster ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/8/muhammad-yunus-sworn-in-as-bangladeshs-interim-leader ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/9/bangladeshi-hindus-rally-amid-fears-of-communal-attacks ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/10/yunus-calls-attacks-on-hindus-heinous-urges-unity ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/13/bangladesh-yunus-visits-hindu-temple-meets-minority-leaders ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/8/20/bangladesh-says-most-post-hasina-attacks-were-political-not-communal ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/9/19/bangladesh-tightens-security-ahead-of-durga-puja ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/10/9/bangladesh-celebrates-durga-puja-under-tight-security ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/25/bangladesh-arrests-hindu-monk-chinmoy-krishna-das-on-sedition-charges ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/26/clashes-erupt-at-chittagong-court-after-monk-bail-denied ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/27/india-deeply-concerned-by-arrest-of-bangladesh-hindu-monk ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/12/2/protesters-storm-bangladesh-mission-in-indian-state-of-tripura ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/12/3/bangladesh-summons-india-envoy-over-tripura-mission-breach ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/12/5/bangladesh-suspends-consular-services-in-agartala ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/12/9/indian-foreign-secretary-misri-meets-yunus-in-dhaka ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/12/10/bangladesh-says-many-viral-claims-of-attacks-on-hindus-debunked . Date approximations per rule 4: Initial reports of attacks (Aug 5) pegged to Hasina's same-day departure; post-fall attacks unfolded over hours-to-days but the first wave is widely dated Aug 5-6 in coverage. Aug 9 rallies and Aug 10 Yunus statement pegged to AJ publication of those reports. Aug 20 'political not communal' framing pegged to Bangladesh police/army briefing per AJ; the underlying internal review continued for weeks. Source disagreements: scale and motive of attacks — AJ's coverage gave significant weight to the interim government's framing that many incidents were politically motivated (targeting Awami-League-affiliated Hindus rather than Hindus as a religious community) and that some viral claims circulating in Indian media had been fact-checked as misleading. The Bangladesh Hindu Buddhist Christian Unity Council reported a much larger figure of attacks; the council's numbers were widely cited in Indian media but were treated cautiously by Bangladesh authorities and independent fact-checkers — entries record both the council's reporting (Aug 6) and Bangladesh's review (Aug 20, Dec 10) on their respective reporting dates. Public figures named: Muhammad Yunus, Chinmoy Krishna Das, Vikram Misri — public officials/figures of record, permitted under rule 6. Saiful Islam Alif (killed lawyer, Nov 26) named because his death is a public legal matter and his name was widely published; permitted under rule 6 as a person whose involvement is a matter of public record. Politically sensitive: cluster spec marks this as politically_sensitive: true; entries written event-anchored, attribute-claims-where-contested, and avoid characterising motive beyond what each side has stated.

Fetched 2026-06-20. Aggregated across CNN's Bangladesh coverage (per user instruction; no single CNN article carries 15+ dated entries on the minority-attacks subtopic). Contributing articles: https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/06/asia/bangladesh-hindu-attacks-hasina-fall-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/08/asia/muhammad-yunus-bangladesh-interim-government-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/09/asia/bangladesh-hindus-rally-protection-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/10/asia/yunus-condemns-hindu-attacks-bangladesh-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/13/asia/yunus-visits-hindu-temple-dhaka-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/20/asia/bangladesh-attacks-political-not-religious-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/10/09/asia/bangladesh-durga-puja-security-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/11/25/asia/bangladesh-hindu-monk-arrested-sedition-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/11/26/asia/chittagong-court-clashes-monk-bail-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/11/26/asia/lawyer-killed-chittagong-court-violence-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/11/27/asia/india-concerned-bangladesh-hindu-monk-arrest-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/11/29/asia/bangladesh-india-statement-internal-affairs-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/12/02/asia/india-tripura-bangladesh-mission-protesters-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/12/03/asia/india-regrets-agartala-bangladesh-mission-incident-intl-hnk ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/12/09/asia/india-foreign-secretary-misri-bangladesh-yunus-intl-hnk . Date approximations per rule 4: Most entries correspond to discrete events with firm dates from CNN reporting. Aug 5 attacks anchored on Hasina's departure day; the wave of attacks unfolded across Aug 5-6 with reporting consolidating later. Source disagreements: scale and motive of attacks — CNN reported the Hindu Buddhist Christian Unity Council's large figures alongside Bangladesh authorities' competing framing that most incidents were political (targeting Awami-League-affiliated individuals) rather than religious. CNN's Aug 6 entry attributes the council's widespread-attacks framing to the council itself rather than treating it as established fact. The Nov 26 lawyer killing — CNN kept the named-victim attribution out of the timeline body to maintain neutrality; the killed lawyer is named in source articles. India-Bangladesh exchanges (Nov 27 Indian concern, Nov 29 Bangladesh response, Dec 3 India regret over Agartala) recorded as official statements on each side. Public figures named: Muhammad Yunus, Chinmoy Krishna Das, Vikram Misri — public officials/figures of record, permitted under rule 6. Politically sensitive: same posture as t1.

disagreements.topic: Scale and motive of post-Aug-5 attacks on Hindus
disagreements.note: The Bangladesh Hindu Buddhist Christian Unity Council reported a large number of attacks on minority homes, businesses and temples in the first days after Hasina's fall; the figure was widely cited, especially in Indian media. Bangladesh police and army subsequently said most incidents were politically motivated (targeting Awami-League-affiliated individuals, who include many Hindus) rather than religiously motivated, and Bangladesh fact-checkers said several viral claims circulating online were debunked or misattributed. Both timelines record the council's reporting on Aug 6 and Bangladesh authorities' framing on Aug 20 / Dec 10 separately, on their respective reporting dates.

On Dec 2 2024 protesters in Agartala entered the Bangladesh assistant high commission compound. AJ described the incident as a breach the Bangladesh side called 'unacceptable' and which led Bangladesh to suspend consular services in Agartala (Dec 5). CNN highlighted India's Dec 3 statement of regret. Both characterisations are consistent; entries reflect each outlet's framing.

disagreements.topic: India-Bangladesh diplomatic exchange over the monk's arrest
disagreements.note: After Chinmoy Krishna Das's Nov 25 arrest, India's foreign ministry said it was deeply concerned (Nov 26 / Nov 27, both dates appear in coverage). Bangladesh said the Indian statement amounted to interference in its internal affairs (Nov 29). CNN's t2 records both sides; AJ's t1 records the Indian concern (Nov 26) and folds the Bangladeshi response into the broader Misri-visit narrative on Dec 9. Not a factual disagreement; a coverage-emphasis difference.
